task_completion.description: Build at least 4 partition walls and install at least 3 doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan in Sweet Home 3D shows new partition walls dividing the open space, with at least 3 door objects placed within or between these walls.

task_completion.description: Define and label at least 4 distinct areas: 'Reception', 'Bullpen', 'Holding Cell', and 'Interrogation'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan shows distinct room areas created (usually shaded or outlined) with text labels clearly reading 'Reception', 'Bullpen', 'Holding Cell', and 'Interrogation'.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Bullpen with at least 6 desks and 8 chairs, and the Reception with at least 4 chairs and 1 desk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, the area labeled 'Bullpen' contains >=6 desks and >=8 chairs. The area labeled 'Reception' contains >=1 desk and >=4 chairs.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Holding Cells with at least 2 beds and 2 toilets, and the Interrogation room with at least 1 table and 2 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, the area labeled 'Holding Cell' contains >=2 beds and >=2 toilets. The area labeled 'Interrogation' contains >=1 table and >=2 chairs.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D photo rendering of the bullpen or holding cells and save it to ~/Desktop/precinct_render.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Create photo' dialog in Sweet Home 3D is used to render a view of the bullpen or holding cells, and a save dialog or terminal command confirms the image is saved exactly as 'precinct_render.png' on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Save the project to preserve all changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title does not show an asterisk (which would indicate unsaved changes), or there is a visible 'Save' dialog/action confirming the project was saved.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the precinct.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application interface being actively used (drawing walls, placing furniture), rather than just terminal commands modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: The 3D rendering was generated using the application's built-in tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Create photo' rendering window in Sweet Home 3D is visible, showing the rendering progress, rather than the agent downloading or copying an external image.

integrity.description: Files were saved through legitimate application features.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard file explorer save dialogs or application menus are used to save the .png and .sh3d files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starter file is 'precinct_starter.sh3d'. The task requires 100 total points to pass, with a threshold of 70. Note a known programmatic defect: the automated verifier fails to count doors because it looks for a 'doorOrWindow' attribute on furniture tags instead of the correct '<doorOrWindow>' tag. The VLM MUST visually verify the presence of at least 3 doors in the screenshots. Additionally, while the automated script expects 35 total furniture items for full points, the prompt only explicitly mandates 26 specific items (7 desks/tables, 14 chairs, 2 beds, 2 toilets). The VLM should score based on the explicit prompt requirements.
